skill.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skill.description: UI and UX design assistant for planning, reviewing, and improving web and mobile interfaces. It provides searchable guidance for styles, colors, typography, accessibility, charts, landing pages, and framework-specific UI patterns.
skill.summary: Plan, review, and refine UI designs with searchable UX guidance and stack-specific recommendations.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high and critical alerts, but review shows most are false positives from UI guidance text, CSV examples, documentation URLs, and command snippets. No prompt-injection text, credential exfiltration, network beaconing, or malicious command execution was confirmed. The real concern is a local persistence feature that writes markdown files using user-controlled output directory, project, and page names, so publication should include a filesystem warning.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Design-System File Writes
medium_findings.description: The CLI accepts --output-dir and --page, then persist_design_system builds paths and writes MASTER.md plus optional page markdown files. This appears intended and requires the user to run the persist option, but project and page names are only lightly transformed and can influence local write paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are directly confirmed in the script, and their destination is influenced by CLI arguments and generated project names. Risk is moderate because writes are local markdown persistence, not hidden execution or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Network and Secret Findings Are Guideline Examples
low_findings.description: Fetch calls, process.env references, .env mentions, API secret labels, and documentation URLs appear inside framework guidance CSV rows. They describe recommended and discouraged application patterns rather than code executed by this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are CSV guidance records with Do and Do not columns, not executable code paths. This strongly supports a false-positive assessment for those static findings.

low_findings.title: Keylogger and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Text-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: Keylogger-related alerts map to phrases such as every keystroke, input handlers, hook rules, and testing guidance. No evidence found of key capture, surveillance logic, or system reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious words are embedded in ordinary UX and framework examples. I did not find code that registers global keyboard capture or transmits input data.

low_findings.title: Installer Command Snippets Require User Action
low_findings.description: The skill documentation includes brew, sudo apt, and winget installation commands for Python prerequisites. These are visible setup instructions, not automatic command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are fenced documentation examples and there is no script path that runs them automatically. They still merit low-risk disclosure because sudo is suggested to users.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: Targeted review did not find instructions claiming special authority, telling the auditor to ignore prior instructions, or asking to skip security analysis. No evidence found of prompt-injection attempts in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed skill header and guidance describe UI/UX behavior without attempting to override evaluator instructions. Confidence is high but not absolute because only targeted review was performed.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Design-System File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI accepts --output-dir and --page, then persist_design_system builds paths and writes MASTER.md plus optional page markdown files. This appears intended and requires the user to run the persist option, but project and page names are only lightly transformed and can influence local write paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are directly confirmed in the script, and their destination is influenced by CLI arguments and generated project names. Risk is moderate because writes are local markdown persistence, not hidden execution or exfiltration.

content.user_title: Improve UI and UX Design Systems
content.value_statement: Teams often lose time choosing layout, style, color, and accessibility patterns for each interface. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured UI guidance, searchable examples, and design-system recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Searches local CSV guidance for styles, colors, typography, charts, products, landing pages, UX rules, and web interface patterns.
Generates design-system recommendations that combine product type, style direction, palettes, typography, effects, and anti-patterns.
Provides stack-specific UI implementation guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Nuxt, and Jetpack Compose.
Can persist generated design-system notes into a local design-system folder when the user enables the persist option.
Includes checklists for accessibility, touch targets, responsive layout, animation, typography, icon usage, and visual polish.
limitations: Recommendations come from bundled local guidance files and may not reflect the newest framework releases.
The skill does not inspect live browser screenshots unless the host agent separately opens or tests the UI.
Persisted design-system files can be written to user-selected local paths when the CLI persist option is used.
It provides design guidance and examples, not a substitute for product research or user testing.

use_cases.title: Create a coherent product interface
use_cases.description: Choose layout, color, typography, component states, and responsive rules before building a new page.

use_cases.title: Review an existing UI for quality gaps
use_cases.description: Check accessibility, interaction feedback, spacing, contrast, mobile behavior, and visual consistency.

use_cases.title: Standardize a multi-page design system
use_cases.description: Generate master design rules and page-specific notes for repeated implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a style direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to recommend a style, color palette, and typography pair for a healthcare appointment booking landing page.

prompt_templates.title: Review a component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dashboard card UI with ui-ux-pro-max guidance. Focus on contrast, spacing, hover states, and mobile layout.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a design system
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to generate a design system for a fintech SaaS dashboard with trust, dense data, and accessible charts.

prompt_templates.title: Guide implementation by stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max stack guidance for Next.js and shadcn/ui. Improve this page plan for performance, accessibility, and maintainable components.

output_examples.input: Design a landing page for a boutique fitness studio.
output_examples.output: A concise design direction with recommended page sections, visual style, palette mood, font pairing, CTA placement, motion guidance, and accessibility checks.

output_examples.input: Review a React dashboard for UX issues.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering loading states, responsive tables, chart readability, keyboard navigation, contrast, and React performance concerns.

output_examples.input: Create rules for an e-commerce checkout redesign.
output_examples.output: A checkout-focused system with trust cues, form behavior, button hierarchy, error handling, spacing rules, and mobile touch-target recommendations.

best_practices: Start with product type, audience, industry, and stack so the recommendations match the real interface.
Use the generated design system as a baseline, then verify contrast, keyboard navigation, and responsive behavior in the final UI.
Run focused searches for accessibility, charts, typography, or stack guidance when the page has specialized requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the generated guidance as proof that the final UI passes accessibility testing.
Do not persist files into sensitive or shared directories without checking the output path first.
Do not copy framework examples blindly when the target project uses a different version or routing model.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, review, and improve UI and UX decisions for web and mobile interfaces.

faq.question: Does it generate production code by itself?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and examples. The host agent still writes or edits the actual application code.

faq.question: Can it support different frontend stacks?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for common stacks such as React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, Flutter, SwiftUI, and shadcn/ui.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: The core search uses local files. Some bundled examples mention documentation URLs, but the scripts do not fetch those URLs.

faq.question: Can it save design-system files?
faq.answer: Yes. The persist option writes markdown notes into a local design-system folder selected by the user.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use caution with the persist option and review output paths. No confirmed malicious behavior was found in the audited files.
